title: Emma Stone Breaks Oscar Records with Bugonia Nominations
text: Emma Stone achieved historic milestones when she received two 2026 Oscar nominations for Bugonia: best actress for her performance and best picture for her producer credit. At 37 years old, Stone became the second-youngest person ever to accumulate seven career Oscar nominations, surpassed only by Walt Disney who reached seven nominations at age 34 in 1936. She is the youngest woman to achieve this distinction, breaking Meryl Streep's previous record of being nominated seven times at age 38 in 1988. Stone also made history as the first woman nominated twice for both producing and acting in a single film. She previously accomplished this dual recognition for 2023's Poor Things, following Frances McDormand's achievement with Nomadland in 2021. Stone's previous five nominations resulted in two wins, both in the best actress category for La La Land and Poor Things. Only three women have won best actress three or more times: Katharine Hepburn with four wins, McDormand with three, and Streep with three.
